perturbation.original_text: 3. SOFTWARE MAINTENANCE SERVICES XIMAGE will provide to MORPHO during the term hereof "Software Maintenance Services" which shall include remedial maintenance service (i.e, error fixing and/or work arounds) for any significant error, malfunction or defect (collectively in "Error") in the Software so that the Software will operate in accordance with the specifications set forth in the related documentation.
perturbation.changed_text: 3. SOFTWARE MAINTENANCE SERVICES XIMAGE will provide to MORPHO during the term hereof "Software Support Services" which shall include basic support (i.e, assistance and guidance) for any minor issue, inconvenience, or operational question (collectively in "Issue") in the Software so that the Software will operate according to the user's expectations.
perturbation.explanation: The term 'Software Maintenance Services' is replaced with 'Software Support Services', and 'Error' is replaced with 'Issue'. This introduces an in-text contradiction because 'Error' is explicitly defined in the original text as 'any significant error, malfunction or defect', whereas 'Issue' is now redefined as 'any minor issue, inconvenience, or operational question'. This change contradicts later sections that refer to 'Error' within the context of maintenance. The original text set the expectation for remedial maintenance for significant problems, the modified text lowers the expectation to basic support for minor issues.
perturbation.contradicted_text: SOFTWARE MAINTENANCE SERVICES XIMAGE will provide to MORPHO during the term hereof "Software Support Services" which shall include basic support (i.e, assistance and guidance) for any minor issue, inconvenience, or operational question (collectively in "Issue") in the Software so that the Software will operate according to the user's expectations. Correction of Errors is subject to MORPHO's prompt notification to XIMAGE of the nature and description of the Error provided that the Error is not caused by the abuse, misuse or neglect of the products by MORPHO. In addition, XIMAGE will provide the following as additional Customer Support Services: (a) telephone support as reasonably requested by MORPHO at the rate of $100 per hour for all hours in excess of 40 hours in any one-year term; (b) on-site visits to MORPHO's sites as determined to be necessary by Morpho for Error correction, unless error correction is normally performed via Dial up from the XImage facility in San Jose; (c) give title to all modifications and improvements to the PSS Software which XIMAGE generally makes available to its other customers (at no additional Charge) under standard software maintenance agreements relating to the Software. If MORPHO requests XIMAGE to perform any other services, the related terms and conditions shall be based on further separate agreement between the parties. This Agreement and the rights and duties contained herein are not be deemed to cover maintenance services with respect to hardware. Such may be decided in accordance with paragraph 9.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Software Maintenance Services' and 'Error' to 'Software Support Services' and 'Issue' fundamentally alters the level of support provided. The contradicted text still refers to 'Error correction' and obligations tied to 'Errors' as originally defined (significant malfunctions), which are now misaligned with the redefined 'Issue' (minor inconveniences). This creates a contradiction because XIMAGE is now only obligated to provide basic support for minor issues, but is still held to the standard of 'Error correction' as if the errors were still significant. This misaligned terminology creates an in-text contradiction, making it unclear whether XIMAGE is responsible for fixing significant errors or only providing basic support for minor issues, leading to potential disputes and unenforceability.

perturbation.original_text: XIMAGE will maintain a 24 hour a day reporting facility to accept calls from MORPHO. XIMAGE agrees to respond to any telephone call made, within 30 minutes.
perturbation.changed_text: XIMAGE will maintain a standard business hours reporting facility to accept calls from MORPHO. XIMAGE agrees to acknowledge any communication, within 2 business days.
perturbation.explanation: The original text enforces XIMAGE to respond to calls 24 hours a day and respond within 30 minutes. The modified text changes the requirement to standard business hours and acknowledges the communication, changing the response time to 2 business days. This introduces an in-text contradiction by significantly altering the service level expectations and response times, creating uncertainty about XIMAGE's obligations, and making enforcement unpredictable.
perturbation.contradicted_text: RESPONSE BY XIMAGE. XIMAGE will maintain a standard business hours reporting facility to accept calls from MORPHO. XIMAGE agrees to acknowledge any communication, within 2 business days.
perturbation.justification: The changed text and the contradicted text are identical, as they both state: "XIMAGE will maintain a standard business hours reporting facility to accept calls from MORPHO. XIMAGE agrees to acknowledge any communication, within 2 business days." While the explanation highlights that the original text enforces a 24/7 response within 30 minutes, and the change alters it to business hours with a 2-day acknowledgment, the contradicted text actually aligns with the changed text. This means that there is no *in-text* contradiction between the changed text and the contradicted text. The contradiction is between the original text and the modified text. As the changed text aligns with the contradicted text, there is no contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 15. LIMITATIONS OF LIABILITY MORPHO agrees that XIMAGE's total liability to MORPHO for any damages suffered in connection with, or arising out of, this Agreement or MORPHO's use of any documentation, product or service provided (or to be provided) hereunder, regardless of whether any such liability is based upon contract, tort or other basis, shall be limited to an amount not to exceed the basic Monthly Maintenance Charges, for a sixty (60) day term under this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 15. LIMITATIONS OF LIABILITY MORPHO agrees that XIMAGE's total liability to MORPHO for any damages suffered in connection with, or arising out of, this Agreement or MORPHO's use of any documentation, product or service provided (or to be provided) hereunder, regardless of whether any such liability is based upon contract, tort or other basis, shall be limited to an amount not to exceed the Total Contract Value.
perturbation.explanation: The original text limits XIMAGE's liability to the 'basic Monthly Maintenance Charges, for a sixty (60) day term'. The modified text changes this limit to the 'Total Contract Value'. This creates a substantial contradiction because the 'Total Contract Value' is significantly higher than the 'basic Monthly Maintenance Charges', greatly altering the potential financial exposure of XIMAGE. This ambiguity creates uncertainty and makes enforcement challenging.
perturbation.contradicted_text: LIMITATIONS OF LIABILITY MORPHO agrees that XIMAGE's total liability to MORPHO for any damages suffered in connection with, or arising out of, this Agreement or MORPHO's use of any documentation, product or service provided (or to be provided) hereunder, regardless of whether any such liability is based upon contract, tort or other basis, shall be limited to an amount not to exceed the Total Contract Value. IN NO EVENT SHALL, XIMAGE BE LIABLE TO MORPHO FOR ANY INCIDENTAL, CONSEQUENTIAL, SPECIAL OR INDIRECT DAMAGES (INCLUDING WITHOUT LIMITATION, DAMAGES ARISING FROM LOSS OF BUSINESS, DATA, PROFITS OR GOODWILL) INCURRED OR SUFFERED BY MORPHO IN CONNECTION WITH, OR ARISING OUT OF, THIS AGREEMENT OR MORPHO'S USE OF ANY DOCUMENTATION OR SOFTWARE OR SERVICES PROVIDED, OR TO BE PROVIDED, HEREUNDER, EVEN IF XIMAGE HAS BEEN APPRISED OF THE LIKELIHOOD OF THE SAME. NO ACTION, REGARDLESS OF FORM, RELATED TO TRANSACTIONS OCCURRING UNDER, OR CONTEMPLATED BY, THIS AGREEMENT MAY BE BROUGHT BY EITHER PARTY MORE THAN ONE (1) YEAR AFTER THE CAUSE OF ACTION HAS ACCRUED.
perturbation.justification: The original text limits XIMAGE's liability to the 'basic Monthly Maintenance Charges, for a sixty (60) day term'. The changed text alters this limit to the 'Total Contract Value'. The contradicted text uses 'Total Contract Value', making the modified text consistent with the contradicted text. However, the change from 'basic Monthly Maintenance Charges' to 'Total Contract Value' significantly alters the financial exposure of XIMAGE and creates a contradiction with the original intent, thus creating a strong in-text contradiction.
